title: New electrochemically synthesized mixed polymers with very high electrochemical stability
abstract:
ADVANCED MATERIALS FOR OPTICS AND ELECTRONICS
Adv[ Mater[ Opt[ Electron[ 09\ 156Ð161 "1999#
Received 0 June 1999
Copyright Þ 1999 John Wiley + Sons\ Ltd[
Accepted 07 September 1999
New Electrochemically Synthesized Mixed
Polymers with Very High Electrochemical
Stability†
Joe¨lle Rault-Berthelot* and Euge`ne Raoult
Synthe`se et Electrosynthe`se Organiques, UMR CNRS 6510, Universite´ de Rennes 1, Campus de Beaulieu,
35042 Rennes cedex, France
The electrosynthesis of new mixed polymers is described, starting with the anodic oxidation of a
mixture of 3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene: EDOT and dicyanomethyleneﬂuorene: DiCNFl. The resulting
mixed polymer possesses a very high electrochemical stability and good electrochromic properties.
Copyright
2000 John Wiley & Sons, Ltd.
KEYWORDS
anodic copolymerization; conducting polymer; p- and n-doping processes; mixed polymers
INTRODUCTION
Up to now\ only a few examples of conducting
polymers with very high electrochemical stability
have been described in the literature[ Very recently\0
the
electrochemistry
of
the
poly"2\3!ethylene!
dioxypyrrole#] poly"EDOP# was reported[ This
polymer is described as a highly stable electro!
chromic polymer and as an aqueous!compatible
conducting polymer[ It is consequently interesting
for biomedical applications[1
Much more work concerns the poly"2\3!ethy!
lenedioxythiophene#] poly"EDOT# whose p!doping
process is stable and reversible in a large potential
range2Ð5 and which also possesses very pronounced
electrochromic properties[ This polymer has been
used to prepare antistatic transparent _lms\4 poly!
meric light!emitting diodes6 and electroplating for
metallization of an insulator[7 This polymer was
obtained either chemically4\8Ð00 or electrochemically

More recently EDOT was involved in copoly!
mers in order to obtain multicoloured electro!
chromic polymers or polyconjugated polymers with
a high spin density polaron state[ For example\

EDOT04Ð06 were anodically obtained starting from
a carbazole unit substituted in position 2 and 5
by two EDOT units[ The interest of the carbazole
unit is the possibility of substitution on the nitrogen
atom[

EXPERIMENTAL
All electrochemical experiments were performed
using a Pt disk electrode "diameter 0 mm#\ the coun!
ter electrode was a vitreous carbon rod and the
reference electrode was a silver wire in a 9[0 M
AgNO2 solution in CH2CN[ All solvents were pur!
chased from SDS with less than 099 ppm of water
and stored under Argon[ Activated Al1O2 was
added in the electrolytic solution to remove excess
moisture[ The three electrode cells were connected
to a PAR Model 062 potentiostat monitored with
a PAR Model 064 signal generator and a PAR
Model 068 signal coulometer[ The cyclic vol!
tammetry traces "CVs# were recorded on an XY
SEFRAM!type TGM 053[ UV spectra were rec!
orded using a Guided Wave model 049 spec!
trophotometer with optical _bres[ IR spectra were
recorded using a spectrophotometer IR!FT from
Nicolet model 194 by di}use re~ectance through
the polymer mixed with KBr[
The sample of monomer EDOT was a kind gift
from the Bayer Society and was used without
puri_cation[ DiCNFl was synthesized as described
previously starting from ~uorenone and malo!
nonitrile[10

two reversible and stable oxido!reduction waves\

0[17 V[ Recurrent sweeps between −9[8 and 0[74 V
allow the rapid growth of a new reversible system

ducting polymer on the electrode[ Study of blue

Finally\ the oxidation of a mixed solution of
EDOT and DiCNFl "Fig[ 0 B\ left column# shows
the oxidation of EDOT "at low potential# followed
by the oxidation of the DiCNFl "at higher poten!
tial#[ Recurrent sweeps allow the growth of a new
reversible Ox:Red system between 9[9 and 0[4 V[
The resulting _lm is highly redox active as seen in
Fig[ 0 B\ right column[ On the CV one can observe
the p!doping process of the poly"EDOT# as a broad
~at plateau from −9[4 V followed by the sharper
poly"DiCNFl# reversible p!doping process at more
anodic potential "¦0[9 to 0[4 V#[ The reduction of
the poly"DiCNFl# is also observed between −9[4
and −0[3 V[ The electro!activity of the new
material extends then from −0[3 to ¦0[4 V and in
this potential range one may observe that there is
no zero current\ the gap of potential between the
oxidation and the reduction of the polymer is
reduced to zero since the polymer is electro!active
The qualities of the mixed polymer are dependent
on the amount of EDOT:DiCNFl in the poly!
merization medium[ EDOT may be added in very
low quantities and the best ratio of EDOT:DiCNFl
is found to be about 0 M:19 M[ The amount of
DiCNFl in the mixed polymer may then also be
controlled by the oxidation potential of the mixed
solution\ Higher potentials correspond to higher
amounts of poly"DiCNFl# versus poly"EDOT#[
What then is the nature of the new polymer< A
true copolymer made by regular coupling between
EDOT and DiCNFl leading to the real copoly
"EDOT:DiCNFl# as in a "Scheme 0#\ a criss!cross of
homopolymer chains as in b or an irregular random
Although we cannot con_rm the structure of this
new material based on the present data\ we choose
to describe the new polymer material as copoly
The stability of the mixed polymer was dem!
onstrated by repetitive cycles in acetonitrile medium
between −0[3 and ¦0[4 V[ The oxidation is more
stable "very high stability of the CVs between −9[4
to 0[4 V# than the reduction "high stability of the
The new materials have also been used as an
electrode modi_er for the reduction of chloranil
and the oxidation of ferrocene between −0[3 and
¦9[6 V[ Fig[ 1 shows\ at the bottom\ the elec!
trochemical behaviour of the two substrates on a
platinum electrode] one reversible ox:red wave at
039 mV for the ferrocene and two reversible red:ox
waves at −359 mV and −0[2 V for the chloranil[
When the platinum is covered by a copoly
"EDOT:DiCNFl# deposit\ the CV shows\ at the top\
the ferrocene oxidation and the chloranil reduction
at the same potential as above\ together with
"EDOT:DiCNFl#[ The _lm is then su.ciently con!
ducting to allow charge transfers in the potential
range between its oxidation and its reduction[ This
was not the case for classical poly~uorenes\ which
conduct only at the threshold potential of their p!
and n!doping processes and with which ferrocene
oxidation and chloranil reduction peaks shift
towards more anodic and cathodic values and
intensity peaks lower\ showing the di}usion of the
substrates through the polymer before its reduction
at the platinum surface[13 CVs of Fig[ 1 show also
that the copoly"EDOT:DiCNFl# catalyses "in terms
of potential shift# the second ferrocene oxidation
wave[ In fact\ the most anodic wave\ observed at
about 0[9 V\ is due to the polymer p!doping process
and to the second oxidation wave of ferrocene with
a potential peak of 0[2 V[ This second wave appears
at 0[6 V on a platinum electrode[ Its intensity
increases on both electrodes with the ferrocene con!

In summary\ anodic oxidation of a mixed solution
of EDOT and DiCNFl\ in experimental conditions
determinated by this study\ leads to the formation
of stable _lms of electrochromic "light blue!grey\
violet or grey!green# materials\ water!compatible
and which also have a very high electrochemical
stability in non!aqueous media[ They are electro!
active in a potential range of 1[8 V "between −0[3
and ¦0[4 V#[ As soon as there is no {zero current|
in that potential range\ the mixed polymer may be
considered as a {zero band!gap| material[ Fur!
thermore\ its conductivity allows its use as an elec!
trode modi_er in a large potential range[ Actually\
the exact structure of the mixed polymer is not
elucidated[ However\ work is continuing on the
synthesis of the parent monomers in order to
materials and to compare their behaviour with the
